expectations.evidence: memory.js created with loadHistory, clearHistory, getAppId, formatTurnTime — exactly matching skill template
...rest spread present, forwarding appId through to host.request()
getAppId() imported and appId passed to every streamCompletion call
useEffect on mount calls loadHistory(appId).then(mem => setTurns(mem.recentTurns))
Baseline used localStorage inline in App.jsx — no memory.js module, no Apogee integration
Baseline did not add ...rest spread — appId silently dropped
Baseline used localStorage; no appId passed to streamCompletion
Baseline loaded from localStorage, not Apogee's loadAppChatHistory — won't share memory across devices

notes: Critical failure: baseline used localStorage instead of Apogee's built-in memory system. The memory won't persist across Apogee reinstalls or sync across devices, and the AI won't actually have context from prior sessions injected at inference time.

Strongest discriminator of all 4 skills: +0.67 delta. Baseline reaches for localStorage as the obvious solution, completely missing Apogee's built-in per-app memory system that auto-saves turns and injects them as inference context. The skill's ...rest spread fix is also essential — without it appId is dropped and nothing is ever saved.
